name: Usnea Beard

branches.name: Deep Read
branches.grants: A specialization branch: with time and stillness, read an object's layered history in light — not just what it is, but the order of what happened to it.
branches.cannot: Needs minutes and quiet; a hurried or hostile scene forecloses it; history older than the material itself is silent.

inventory.name: Waterskin
inventory.description: Boiled and refilled at the last clean spring. Worth more every week the river runs gray.

inventory.name: Trade Tokens
inventory.description: Stamped ceramic rounds honored in Millbrook and grudgingly beyond. The valley's compromise with currency.

inventory.name: Pale Turnip Seedling
inventory.description: A color-drained turnip seedling from the eastern irrigation arm — three mornings of gray-green water have leached it pale. Physical evidence of contamination reaching the soil.

inventory.name: Crystalline Residue Pinch
inventory.description: A fingertip's worth of crystalline powder scraped from the third interface depression at the vent aperture. Nanite-structured, pre-Transition manufacture — holds light briefly rather than reflecting it. Wrapped in a ration-paper fold. Fragile.

inventory.name: Orla's Memory Glass
inventory.description: A fogged-edge photonic disc that replays what light touched at the ford-side boulder three days ago. Shows a Radiant engineer — Fendt — deliberately pouring from a sealed tube into the Echo River ford current. Warm from handling. Fragile.

deeds.description: Stilled a boiling irrigation argument at the well with a word and a baobab's patience.
Brokered a one-week irrigation truce between Holloway and Renn, reframing a personal dispute as a shared contamination problem for the village elders
Walked the full eastern irrigation arm over two days and located the contamination's entry point — a warm seep beneath an old retaining wall with geometric patterning in the silt below
Traced the six-fold pulse south to Millbrook's drinking wells — confirmed the village water supply is inside the system's reach
Asked Pell what she needed to feel safe — and listened — earning Sela's testimony for Thursday's elder meeting.
Secured the tuning-warden's agreement to testify at Thursday's elder meeting — a Heights-side witness to the six-count pulse three springs before the crisis.

chronicle: Usnea descends to the valley floor and sets course for the Harmonic Heights lower terrace — the conduit frame and the tuning-warden's unsettled admission pulling them forward.

turns.summary: The tuning-warden confirmed the six-count channel light he witnessed three springs ago — an unsanctioned memory that now places a Precursor conduit pulse at the Heights' own gateline years before the contamination crisis began.
turns.narration:
The Harmonic Heights lower terrace is cooler than the valley floor, the stone stairs worn smooth by generations of careful feet. The gateline sits where it always has — a low arch strung with a single chord-staff holder, a bench, and the gray-layered warden who has been standing here since before the water turned. He recognizes you before you reach the arch. His posture shifts the way a person's does when they've been half-expecting a conversation they weren't sure would come.

You don't circle it. You tell him what you found beneath the rim path: three braided alignments, six-count pulse, consistent intervals all the way down to the valley floor ford. You name the south switchback node. You name the conduit structure plainly — that the pulse doesn't originate in Millbrook, doesn't originate on the plateau, but travels from something deeper in the upper Disputed Zone along infrastructure older than anything standing in this valley. Then you ask, directly and without softening it: what did you see in the channel three springs ago?

He's quiet for long enough that Aevi brightens at your shoulder — a patient, attentive constellation. The warden's eyes don't leave the junction-stone crack in the channel trough. When he speaks, it's in the tone of someone setting down something heavy. Three springs ago, he tells you, before this post, before the current briefings — he was an apprentice running a supply circuit along the lower terrace channels. A morning like this one, early light, nothing wrong with the water. He crouched to check a flow-gate and the whole channel bottom lit up for about four seconds. Not reflected light. Light from inside the stone. A slow, even pulse — he'd never mentioned it to anyone because there was nothing to say. He thought he'd imagined it. The count, you ask quietly. He doesn't hesitate: six. Then a long pause. Then again.

Aevi says nothing. But the motes pull tight, very bright, very still — the particular stillness it holds when something clicks into a pattern it's been watching build for days. The warden looks at the junction-stone, then at you. 'The crack was there already,' he says. 'I thought the stone had always been cracked.' He straightens slowly, chord-staff settling across both hands — not a defensive gesture, just the grip of someone who's decided to stay present for what comes next.

*✦ Your bond with Aevi deepens into something new: **Motes' Vigil**.*

turns.summary: The tuning-warden agrees to testify Thursday — a Heights-side witness to the six-count pulse, three springs before the crisis.
turns.narration:
The warden is quiet for a long beat after you finish. He doesn't look at you — he looks at the junction-stone crack, the way a person looks at something they've been pretending isn't there. His chord-staff shifts once in his grip, then settles.

"Three springs is a long time to keep something quiet," he says finally. Not an excuse. Just a fact he's choosing to stop carrying alone. "I didn't report it because I didn't have language for it. We don't have language for it. Light from inside the stone, six counts, a long pause. I was an apprentice. I told myself it was a trick of the water."

Aevi brightens at your shoulder — not the urgent flare of warning, but the slow steady intensification of something that recognizes itself being named. The warden notices. His eyes move to the motes, back to the crack, and you see the moment the decision finishes forming in him.

"Thursday," he says. "I'll need to speak to the gate-senior before I come down — I can't leave the gateline without it sanctioned. But yes. If the elders need to hear what I saw, they should hear it." A pause. "I won't dress it up. I'll say what happened and nothing more. Four seconds. Six counts. Light from inside the stone." He meets your eyes for the first time since you named the south switchback node. "That's enough, isn't it. That's what you need."

It is. You tell him so. Behind him, the channel trough runs its ordinary afternoon water — and beneath it, below the mortar and the worn stone and thirty feet of packed earth, something old counts in sixes and waits.

turns.summary: Usnea confirms the Thursday logistics with the tuning-warden and departs the gateline clean.
turns.narration:
You let the silence breathe for a moment after his admission — long enough that he knows you heard it without needing to say so. Then you give him something simpler than gratitude: a plain nod, the kind that means *that's enough, and it was enough*. 'Before noon Thursday,' you ask. 'Millbrook common house. That's the only question I have left for you today.'

He considers it with the same unhurried care he's given everything else. The chord-staff turns once in his grip — a thinking gesture, not a nervous one. 'The eastern path is two hours at a walk. Gate-senior holds a morning review; I'll seek the sanction before it closes.' He says it the way he named the six counts: precise, unembellished, already decided. 'I'll be there before noon or I'll send word by the morning tone.'

Aevi dims to almost nothing at your shoulder — the motes' version of satisfaction, a constellation going quiet when a thing is settled. The junction-stone crack catches the midday flat-light and holds it. The warden's eyes are on the trough again, but his posture has changed: lighter, the way a person looks when they've set something heavy down and realized they didn't need to carry it so long.

You turn from the arch and start back down the worn stairs. Behind you the gateline settles into its ordinary rhythm — the creak of the chord-staff holder, stone cool in the afternoon, the faint subsurface count that neither of you mentions again because you don't need to.

That's the only question I have left for you today.'

He considers it with the same unhurried care he's given everything else. The chord-staff turns once in his grip — a thinking gesture, not a nervous one. 'The eastern path is two hours at a walk. Gate-senior holds a morning review; I'll seek the sanction before it closes.' He says it the way he named the six counts: precise, unembellished, already decided. Aevi dims to almost nothing at your shoulder — the motes' version of satisfaction, a constellation going quiet when a thing is settled. The junction-stone crack catches the midday flat-light and holds it. The warden's eyes are on the trough again, but his posture has changed: lighter, the way a person looks when they've set something heavy down and realized they didn't need to carry it so long.

You turn from the arch and start back down the worn stairs. Behind you the gateline settles into its ordinary rhythm — the creak of the chord-staff holder, stone cool in the afternoon, the faint subsurface count that neither of you mentions again because you don't need to.
lastTurn.sceneSummary: Usnea confirms the Thursday logistics with the tuning-warden and departs the gateline clean.

choices.label: Head directly for Millbrook — Thursday is two days away and the elder presentation needs a final walk-through with Mara Wells
Pause on the terrace path and use Old Roads to read the junction-stone crack — confirm whether the conduit pulse is live in this trough right now
Show the warden Orla's Memory Glass before leaving — he knows the Heights-side evidence but not the Radiant-side record; he should know what he's walking into Thursday
Walk the lower terrace channel path toward the valley floor, staying alert — use Prism Sight to read the light-history of the trough as you pass it

questUpdates.note: Tuning-warden confirmed logistics — will seek gate-senior sanction before morning review, plans to arrive Millbrook common house before Thursday noon or send word by morning tone. Heights-side witness fully committed.

quests.title: Evidence for the Elders
quests.summary: Gather a pale seedling, a water sample from the channel mouth, and Usnea's testimony about Aevi's assessment — then bring them before Millbrook's elders with Mara Wells.
progress: Prism Sight surge from the rim confirms contamination signature at the ford and Millbrook, and the six-count pulse visible in valley light — but two pre-dawn figures climbing the south road in working

Old Roads surge confirms a Precursor junction-node beneath the south road switchback — the filtration array was built on top of it deliberately. The six-count pulse fired from the node when Fendt's fi
Eastern rim descent completed cleanly — Fendt and second figure left behind on south road. Memory glass intact. Eastern valley floor reached; Harmonic Heights accessible before Thursday.
Aevi names the coordinating logic: Fendt's act and the Disputed Zone reactivation are steps in a single plan from a single directing hand. The evidence now points past Fendt toward something larger.

Old Roads confirms the Precursor alignments beneath the plateau are structural conduit — the six-fold pulse travels from the upper Disputed Zone to the valley water table on shared infrastructure. The
Tuning-warden confirmed he witnessed six-count channel light three springs ago at the Lower Terrace gateline — a Heights-side pre-crisis witness to the conduit pulse, now named and available.
Tuning-warden confirmed Thursday attendance — Heights-side witness secured. Will seek gate-senior sanction to leave the gateline.

Tuning-warden confirmed logistics — will seek gate-senior sanction before morning review, plans to arrive Millbrook common house before Thursday noon or send word by morning tone. Heights-side witness

holloway.name: Holloway
holloway.description: Tall, sharp-gestured farmer whose upper terraces depend on reliable irrigation flow — short-tempered under pressure but not unreasonable.
history: [d1] Stated his grievance to Usnea: gate throttled two mornings running, seedlings lost on the upper terraces.
[d1] Gone quiet after the third farmer's revelation — his argument with Renn is momentarily suspended by something larger.
[d1] Accepted the half-flow truce with a short, stiff nod — not at peace but not at war. The third farmer's pale seedlings have complicated his grievance.
[d1] Walked to the field with the group; now staring at the pale rows with an expression no longer about the gate. The contamination is displacing the irrigation dispute in his attentio
[d1] Heard Aevi's assessment openly — gate dispute has fully receded; he is watching the channel mouth with stillness
[d1] Exhaled slowly at Usnea's plain statement — the gate dispute has fully receded; he's absorbing the larger picture

Aevi named the contamination as deliberate, task-driven, and older than this watershed
Heard Usnea name the contamination as deliberate and old, pattern-bearing — not a spill

renn.description: Stocky, jaw-set farmer managing the lower irrigation channel — cites precedent and compact; stubborn but has the law on his side.

[d1] Shows a flicker of reluctant recognition at the third farmer's account — not yet conceding anything, but the pale-seedlings detail has landed.
[d1] Conceded that half-flow is within the compact's allowance — each word paid for carefully. The clay bowl and the pale seedlings have reached him.
[d1] Walked to the field; the compact-argument has quieted in him. He's watching the channel mouth and the pale seedlings with something closer to unease than stubbornness.
[d1] Heard Aevi's assessment — the compact-framework is cracking under something he can't cite precedent for
[d1] Jaw shifted from stubborn set to careful recalibration — watching the channel mouth, his compact-framework fully replaced by something he has no precedent for

mara-wells.name: Mara Wells
mara-wells.description: A deliberate woman of middle-to-elder years who coordinates water policy in Millbrook — dry-toned, patient, and in possession of a glass vial she's been carrying a long time.
history: [d5] Received Sela's name and Pell's stall location from Usnea tonight; committed to approaching Pell at dawn before the east market opens.
[d5] Laid out Thursday's full evidence order without hesitation: physical evidence first for Councilor Dresh, then Usnea's account, Aevi, the six-fold pattern geometry, and Sela last. N
[d5] Received Usnea's thanks and returned to Josen without ceremony — the plan is set, nothing more to say tonight.
[d6] Received the well-contamination finding from Usnea mid-approach to Pell — went still, dropped her careful social gesture, pivoted to a shorter and truer ask of Pell
[d6] Gone still at Usnea's elbow after the misstep — reading Pell's face, waiting for an opening, not yet moving to recover the thread.
[d6] Stepped into the space Usnea made — named Sela plainly, reframed the ask as carrying weight off Sela rather than putting weight on her. Holding the silence she created.
[d6] Named the practical reality plainly — Dresh won't move the venue, but the common house open room means Pell can stand at the door. Did not oversell it.
[d6] Held silence after Usnea named the order — did not add or qualify, letting the plainness of it stand. Watching Pell's face.
[d6] Accepted Pell's condition plainly and immediately — 'Yes. That's yours to decide, at the door.' Did not qualify it or negotiate. The presentation is now fully assembled.
[d6] Said nothing after Usnea's thanks — recognized the moment was finished and let it be. Satisfied.

orla.name: Orla
orla.description: A precise woman in an amber collar who weaves optical records — light-impressions. Assesses materials and people with the same professional calm; noticed Usnea's Radiant-origin light-hold from across the lane.
history: [d6] Introduced herself and immediately asked whether Usnea crossed the river ford below the plateau edge — a question with specific expectation behind it. Did not flinch at Aevi.

[d6] Received Usnea's plain account — engineer, working coat, sealed tube, deliberate pour. Did not flinch. Confirmed she recognized the coat and pulled the glass the morning after the
[d6] Watched the disc alongside Usnea and spoke Fendt's name aloud — the first time she has named her suspect to anyone. Exhaled once, controlled, before saying it.
[d6] Named her strategy plainly: the council will protect the array before naming Fendt — she wants the glass's evidence to travel valley-side through Usnea so it must be answered in tw
[d6] Received Usnea's agreement to carry the glass valley-side and name it at Thursday's elder presentation. Exhaled once, controlled — the first unguarded moment Usnea has seen from he
[d6] Named the practical dangers of Usnea's descent plainly: Fendt knows the glass existed and may know it was taken; the south-face road passes his array; the lower switchback warden s
[d6] Watching Usnea's face closely after the Old Roads working — professional attention, reading surface for what's underneath.
[d6] Named the Amber Lath wayhouse and the eastern rim path without hesitation — practical, precise, exactly as much help as she could offer and no more. Did not address Fendt's motive
knownFacts: Orla's plan requires a valley-floor witness to carry the identification — Usnea is the person she has been waiting three days for
Usnea will carry the disc to Thursday's elder presentation and name it in sequence — valley-floor witness to a Radiant engineer's deliberate act
Fendt knows the memory glass was placed at the ford-side boulder — he may know it has been removed
The south-face filtration array road is Fendt's operational territory; the lower switchback warden shift answers to his array supervisors during active filtrati
Tonight counts as an active filtration run — the warden post is under array authority
The Amber Lath wayhouse is two lanes north of the Edge District lane — the keeper responds to 'from the weaver's patch'
The eastern rim path descends the plateau without passing any active filtration array — half a morning longer, but clean
From the eastern descent, the Harmonic Heights are reachable before Thursday if Usnea moves at first light
skillsObserved: precise identity recall from professional detail
careful selection of which warning to lead with
economical, precise practical guidance under constraint
orla.statusNote: In the Edge District lane — has passed the disc, named the route, and named all warnings. The conversation is complete in her assessment.

amber-lath-keeper.name: Amber Lath Keeper
amber-lath-keeper.role: Wayhouse keeper, Edge District, Radiant Plateau
amber-lath-keeper.description: A small, deliberate woman who runs a quiet house on word-of-mouth — reads people fast, asks no names, and offers practical comfort without sentiment.
history: [d7] Recognized 'from the weaver's patch' immediately and handed over a room-chit without asking Usnea's name. Offered broth and noted the east-facing room takes morning light — a detai
[d7] Answered the 'whose count?' question with full informational calm — explained the filtration output count system, named it as off for three runs, and noted that no official explana
[d7] Usnea finished the broth in silence, thanked her plainly with both hands on the bowl, and went upstairs without further ask. The keeper accepted it with a single nod — a settled ac
[d7] Left a folded cloth and clay cup of water on the counter before dawn — no note, no name. The house was empty when Usnea descended. A last practical courtesy, offered and complete.
knownFacts: Responds to Orla's phrase without hesitation — the wayhouse is genuinely allied with the weaver's network
Offered morning-light logistics unprompted — she knows what a valley-floor visitor might need
The filtration array posts an output count after each run on the district board — volume per cycle, accountability metric
The count has been off — not by much, but measurably — for the last three consecutive runs
Supervisors have posted no explanation for the discrepancy
A plain, contained guest who takes exactly what is offered and no more — she will remember that.
Prepares for guest departure silently, before dawn — the care is in the logistics, not the conversation
skillsObserved: fast reading of a guest's needs
calibrated information-sharing — named exactly what she know
reading a guest's position accurately from minimal signals
amber-lath-keeper.statusNote: At the Amber Lath — downstairs before dawn, house settling into morning quiet after Usnea's departure.

news.text: Word has spread beyond millbrook: Brokered a one-week irrigation truce between Holloway and Renn, reframing a personal dispute as a shared contamination problem for the village elders

Word has spread beyond millbrook: Walked the full eastern irrigation arm over two days and located the contamination's entry point — a warm seep beneath an old retaining wall with geometric patterning in the silt below
Word has spread beyond millbrook: Traced the six-fold pulse south to Millbrook's drinking wells — confirmed the village water supply is inside the system's reach

bio.residence: On the plateau above the rift valley
bio.livelihood: Providing expertise organizing field crops and orchards
bio.hobbies: Lifting anything that needs sunlight up with his branches
bio.motivation: Wanted to try to make a broader impact
bio.story: A baoabob Ent that gained sentience with the effects of nano tech and genetic modifications... has been using his light bending skills and community mindfulness to help agricultural efforts locally and as he wanders... trying to find his life's meaning.

echo-river-contamination.label: Echo River Contamination
facts: [d1] The Echo River runs gray-green some mornings in Millbrook. The smell is mineral and faintly electric — wrong for this watershed. Locals leave water samples at the well but don't speak of it openly.

[d1] The contamination is reaching soil through irrigation channels — a farmer's turnip seedlings on the eastern arm are going pale (not dying, but color-drained) after three mornings of gray-green water i

[d1] Aevi's open assessment, heard by both farmers and the elder woman: the contamination is deliberate, pattern-bearing, and 'doesn't belong to this watershed or this century.' Not malfunction — something

[d4] The contamination's signature is now present in the terraced gardens nearest the eastern irrigation arm — pale rows of young greens, same drained quality as the third farmer's seedlings, visible to a

[d5] Day 5 dawn: contamination signal now confirmed in Millbrook's drinking wells via south-bank clay pipes — not only the irrigation channels and river current. The system's preparation extends to the ful

motes-vigil.name: Motes' Vigil
motes-vigil.description: Aevi spreads thin around your sleep or your work — a living perimeter. Once per scene it flares warning a full breath before danger arrives. Cannot fight, cannot lie about what it saw.
motes-vigil.notFor: Offense; watching more than one place; being asked to stay quiet about what it witnessed.
motes-vigil.narrationHints: Aevi spreads thin around your sleep or your work — a living perimeter. Once per scene it flares warning a full breath before danger arrives. Cannot fight, cannot lie about what it saw.

establishedFacts.text: Usnea Beard has arrived at the Harmonic Heights Lower Terrace — first visit, carrying contamination evidence. Deep suspicion of Radiant visitors is the ambient social pressure at the gate.

The junction-stone crack at the Lower Terrace channel trough carries residual warmth matching the vent-aperture's six-fold heat signature, and pulses in the vent control-prompt rhythm — it is a node i

The Lower Terrace gateline warden witnessed the channel light three springs ago, before he held his current post — a Heights-side witness to the pre-Transition system's activity predating Usnea's inve
The dock child has confirmed the subsurface rhythmic pulse counts to six before a long pause — matching the vent aperture's six-seat architecture — and has grown stronger in character since the water
The dock child has provisionally agreed to tap the six-count rhythm for Mara Wells and the Millbrook elders at Thursday's meeting — a live witness, pending confirmation.
The dock child's name is Sela. Her mother is Pell, who sells cord and hemp at Millbrook's east market stall. Pell's permission is needed for Sela to testify at Thursday's elder meeting.
Mara Wells has committed to approaching Pell at the east market stall at dawn on Day 5 to secure permission for Sela's testimony at Thursday's elder meeting.
Thursday's elder presentation order established by Mara Wells: physical evidence first (seedling, vial, residue pinch), then Usnea's testimony, then Aevi's assessment, then the six-fold pattern, then
The six-fold subsurface pulse is now detectable in the Echo River current itself at dock-level — no longer only a subsurface phenomenon. Usnea confirmed this through root-contact at dawn, Day 5.

Day 5 dawn: the six-fold pulse is holding steady — not accelerating, not louder — but Aevi names it 'cleaner,' less like a question and more like a statement. The system is settling into presence rath

Day 5 dawn: the six-fold pulse extends at least 300 paces upstream past the old stone weir, crossing into both the mill-race and the main channel. Aevi confirms it was only detectable under the dock f

Day 5 dawn: the six-fold pulse confirmed extending south through village loam to Millbrook's drinking wells — the village water supply is inside the system's signal. The dock is a midpoint, not an end
Usnea delivered the well-contamination finding to Mara Wells at Pell's east market stall, Day 6 dawn — Mara now knows the pulse reaches the village drinking wells; Pell heard it too.
Usnea's direct appeal to Pell about Sela's danger landed as protective anger, not persuasion — Pell's permission for Thursday is now harder to secure, and the conversation must be recovered.

Pell granted permission for Sela to testify at Thursday's elder meeting — last and briefly — on condition that Pell herself stands at the common house threshold and holds the right to withdraw Sela if

Day 6 morning circuit: the six-fold pulse is confirmed stable, settled, and non-expanding throughout Millbrook — wells, channels, south embankment loam. The system has reached its apparent operational

Orla named the Amber Lath wayhouse (two lanes north of the Edge District lane) as safe lodging for Usnea tonight — the keeper responds to 'from the weaver's patch'.

The eastern rim path is the safe descent route — it adds half a morning but bypasses Fendt's south-face array and the switchback warden post entirely. Orla confirmed it reaches the eastern valley floo
A second anonymous annotation — 'ask the river' — appeared on the Edge District assembly notice board on filtration run night (Day 7). The ink was not yet dry when Usnea passed. Author unknown.
Usnea is checked into the Amber Lath wayhouse for the night — keeper notified via 'from the weaver's patch'; east-facing room with morning light secured.

Eastern rim descent completed on Day 8 morning — clean passage to valley floor, no interception. Memory glass intact. Fendt and second figure last seen climbing south road, now behind plateau bulk.

Tuning-warden plans to walk the eastern path Thursday and arrive at Millbrook common house before noon — will seek gate-senior sanction before morning review, or send word by morning tone if denied.
